root_cause.text: The application name extracted from an uploaded archive is not properly sanitized, allowing directory traversal characters (such as `/`) to be used in file path construction, which enables attackers to write or delete files outside the intended application directory.
root_cause.evidence: The patch introduces a `filterAppNameForFilesystem` method that replaces `/` with `^` and applies it to the application name (`appName` or `desc.name()`) when constructing `File` objects within `ApplicationArchive.java`.

vuln_statements.content: return new File(new File(appsDir, appName), fileName);

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The application name extracted from an uploaded archive is not properly sanitized, allowing directory traversal characters (such as `/`) to be used in file path construction, which enables attackers to write or delete files outside the intended application directory.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch introduces a `filterAppNameForFilesystem` method that replaces `/` with `^` and applies it to the application name (`appName` or `desc.name()`) when constructing `File` objects within `ApplicationArchive.java`.

intent_record.intent_summary: Sanitize application names to prevent path traversal when building filesystem paths.
intent_record.what: Added a helper method to replace slashes with carets in application names and applied it to the purgeApplication method.
intent_record.how: Prevents path traversal issues by ensuring the application name does not contain directory separators, keeping the file operation constrained to the intended directory.

intent_record.intent_summary: Sanitize the application name to prevent filesystem path vulnerabilities.
intent_record.what: Wrapped the application name with a filesystem filter function.
intent_record.how: Sanitizes the application name before using it to create a directory path.

intent_record.intent_summary: Sanitize the application name to ensure it is safe for use in filesystem paths.
intent_record.what: The application name is now filtered using a specific method before being used to construct a file name.
intent_record.how: This ensures that any invalid characters in the application name are handled properly, preventing filesystem errors during file creation.

intent_record.intent_summary: Sanitize the application name before using it in file path construction to ensure filesystem safety.
intent_record.what: The appName variable is now passed through the filterAppNameForFilesystem method before being used in the File constructor.
intent_record.how: This sanitizes the application name to ensure it is safe to use in the filesystem path, preventing potential path traversal or invalid character errors.

grouping_result.intent: Sanitize the application name before using it in file path construction to ensure filesystem safety.

consistency_review.core_intent: Sanitize application names before using them in filesystem path construction to prevent path traversal vulnerabilities.

relevance_review.reason: The group's intent and hunk summaries explicitly address the root cause by sanitizing application names to prevent path traversal during file path construction.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 62ad95f3f1b7dfab11ba291dc25eb8c95d80dcc8
Author: Thomas Vachuska <tom@onlab.us>
Date:   Wed Feb 18 12:11:36 2015 -0800

    Added ability to upload apps as both app.xml or app.zip.
    Added a number of app.xml files for built-in apps.
    Added ability to install & activate in one command.
    
    Change-Id: I3fa5fa487ef76d9fe3da4d6dce8045d538cba423

diff --git a/core/common/src/main/java/org/onosproject/common/app/ApplicationArchive.java b/core/common/src/main/java/org/onosproject/comm
bic_trace_step.reason: The functions like `purgeApplication`, `getApplicationInputStream`, and `expandPlainApplication` construct file paths using `appName` (or `desc.name()`) directly, such as `File appDir = new File(appsDir, appName);` and `File file = appFile(desc.name(), APP_XML);`. There is no sanitization of the application name, which allows for directory traversal.

diff --git a/core/common/src/main/java/org/onosproject/common/app/ApplicationArchive.java b/core/common/src/main/java/org/onosproject/common/app/ApplicationArchive.java
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000..90e6005145
--- /dev/null
++
bic_trace_step.reason: The application name is extracted from the XML configuration without sanitization in `loadAppDescription` (`String name = cfg.getString(NAME);`) and directly used to construct file paths in `expandApplication` (`File appDir = new File(appsDir, desc.name());`) and `saveApplication`, making it vulnerable to directory traversal attacks.

bic_trace_step.reason: The `ApplicationArchive.java` file contains the vulnerable logic in `expandApplication` and `appFile`. The application name extracted from the archive descriptor is directly used in file path construction: `File appDir = new File(appsDir, desc.name());` and `File file = new File(appDir, entry.getName());` without any sanitization, which allows directory traversal.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 02aeb0339f584153798906c5f3a1c2809ead511e
Author: Thomas Vachuska <tom@onlab.us>
Date:   Tue Jan 6 22:36:30 2015 -0800

    ONOS-542 Defining application subsystem interfaces & public constructs.
    
    Change-Id: Iba0d2cb69dace5beee8a68def9918059ce755b5c

diff --git a/core/common/src/main/java/org/onosproject/common/app/ApplicationArchive.java b/core/common/src/main/java/org/onosproject/common/app/ApplicationArchive.java
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000..90e6005145
--- /dev/null
++
bic_trace_step.reason: The function `loadAppDescription` extracts the application name directly from the XML configuration without sanitization (`String name = cfg.getString(NAME);`). This name is then used in `expandApplication` (`File appDir = new File(appsDir, desc.name());`) and `saveApplication` (`appFile(desc.name(), desc.name() + ".zip")`) to construct file paths, which allows directory traversal attacks if the name contains characters like `../`.
